query: Create a comprehensive security audit checklist for a FinTech app handling transactions up to $1M — cover OWASP, PCI-DSS, fraud detection, and regulatory requirements

gnn.output:
# Comprehensive Security Audit Checklist for High-Value FinTech Applications

## Executive Summary & Scope Definition

This unified report synthesizes technical and business security perspectives to produce a comprehensive audit framework for FinTech applications processing transactions up to **$1,000,000 USD** per single transaction. At this transaction threshold, organizations face mandatory **PCI-DSS Level 1** compliance requirements—triggered by processing volumes exceeding 6 million card transactions annually or when breach potential could result in significant financial fraud. Additionally, this transaction ceiling activates enhanced regulatory scrutiny across multiple jurisdictions, including AML/BSA reporting obligations, state money transmitter licensing requirements, and EU PSD2 compliance for organizations serving European customers.

The technical perspective emphasizes granular testing methodologies, specific tool requirements (Burp Suite Professional v2023.2+, OWASP ZAP, SQLMap, Ffuf), and precise pass/fail criteria for security controls. The business perspective focuses on compliance mapping, tool cost considerations, and operational implementation frameworks. Where these perspectives diverge in emphasis or approach, this report explicitly acknowledges the divergence and provides rationale for recommended alignment.

**Query Requirements Addressed by This Report:**

| Requirement | Section Reference |
|-------------|-------------------|
| OWASP compliance framework | Section 4: OWASP Framework Implementation |
| PCI-DSS Level 1 requirements | Section 3: Regulatory & Compliance Framework |
| Fraud detection requirements | Section 5: Fraud Detection Architecture |
| Regulatory requirements (AML/BSA, state licensing, PSD2) | Section 3: Regulatory & Compliance Framework |
| Transaction processing up to $1M | Section 2: Scope Definition |
| Real-time authorization SLAs | Section 5.2: Authorization Performance Requirements |

---

## Section 1: Critical Ris

fixed.output:
# Comprehensive Security Audit Checklist for High-Value FinTech Transactions

## Executive Summary

This security audit checklist is designed for FinTech applications processing transactions up to $1 million. It integrates requirements from OWASP, PCI-DSS v4.0, fraud detection best practices, and major regulatory frameworks including GDPR, SOX, and AML/KYC mandates.

**Scope:** All application components, APIs, mobile applications, backend services, databases, and third-party integrations processing or storing payment data.

**Assumptions:**

- Transaction limit: Up to $1,000,000 per transaction
- Card-present and card-not-present payment flows
- Multi-jurisdictional operations
- User authentication required

---

## 1. Security Governance & Management

### 1.1 Security Policies

| # | Control | Description | Priority | Mapping | Numeric Threshold |
|---|---------|-------------|----------|---------|-------------------|
| 1.1.1 | Security Charter | Formal information security policy document approved by board/executive leadership | Critical | PCI-DSS 12.1, SOX, GLBA | — |
| 1.1.2 | Data Classification | Documented classification scheme (Public, Internal, Confidential, Restricted) applied to all data | Critical | PCI-DSS 12.3, GDPR Art. 5 | — |
| 1.1.3 | Acceptable Use Policy | Defined acceptable use guidelines for systems and data access | High | PCI-DSS 12.3.1, SOX | — |
| 1.1.4 | Password Policy | Enterprise-wide minimum 12-character passwords with complexity requirements | Critical | PCI-DSS 8.2, OWASP | — |
| 1.1.5 | Encryption Standards | Documented standards requiring AES-256 for data at rest, TLS 1.2+ for data in transit | Critical | PCI-DSS 3.4, 4.1 | Key rotation: **Annual minimum** |
| 1.1.6 | Retention Policy | Defined data retention periods aligned with regulatory requirements (see Section 1.1.6a) | Critical | PCI-DSS 3.1, SOX, FINRA | See detailed table below |
| 1.1.7 | Policy Review Cycle | Annual policy review with documented approvals | High | PCI-DS
